---
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    papersize: letter
    geometry:
      - top=1in
      - bottom=1in
      - left=1.25in
      - right=1.25in
    fontsize: 11pt
    mainfont: "TeX Gyre Pagella"
    sansfont: "TeX Gyre Heros"
    linestretch: 1.15
    number-sections: false
    toc: false
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: NavyBlue
    urlcolor: NavyBlue
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \usepackage{lineno}
        \usepackage{ragged2e}
        \pagenumbering{gobble}
        \renewcommand\linenumberfont{\normalfont\small}
---

```{=latex}
\begin{flushright}
Adopted: XX/XX/20XX
\end{flushright}

\begin{center}
{\Large\bfseries ACADEMIC SENATE}\\[4pt]
of\\[4pt]
{\large\bfseries CALIFORNIA POLYTECHNIC STATE UNIVERSITY}\\[4pt]
San Luis Obispo, CA\\[1.5em]
\textbf{AS-XXX-26}\\[1em]
{\large\bfseries RESOLUTION ON THE STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCE SURVEY}
\end{center}

\vspace{1.5em}

\begin{linenumbers}
\noindent
WHEREAS,\quad The Collective Bargaining Agreement states that ``[w]ritten or electronic student questionnaire evaluations shall be required for all faculty unit employees who teach'' (15.15); and

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
WHEREAS,\quad The Collective Bargaining Agreement states that periodic evaluation review of tenured, tenure-line, and temporary faculty unit employees will include student evaluations (15.23, 15.28--29, 15.32, and 15.34); and

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
WHEREAS,\quad The CSU, CSU Academic Senate, and CFA Joint Committee ``Report on Student Evaluations'' (March 12, 2008) recommended that ``[c]ampuses should use a well-designed student evaluation instrument (with demonstrable validity and reliability) in providing diagnostic information and feedback, and those involved in evaluations should have an understanding of their formative as well as summative uses'' (p.~9); and

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
WHEREAS,\quad The ``Report on Student Evaluations'' stated that ``[t]he faculty on each individual campus have the right, through their governance process, to develop the campus-based program of student evaluations of teaching'' (p.~7); and

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
WHEREAS,\quad AS-759-13 (April 16, 2013) established the current university-wide student evaluation questions, scale, and metric, and designated the Instruction and Faculty Affairs Committees as the appropriate committees for future revisions; and

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
WHEREAS,\quad ASI Resolution \#25-04 (2025) called for reform of the student evaluation instrument and processes, citing concerns about bias, validity, and the impact of evaluations on faculty from underrepresented groups; and

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
WHEREAS,\quad The Academic Senate established the Ad Hoc Committee on Student Perception of Teaching Effectiveness, charged with providing a revised policy and resolution to replace AS-759-13; and

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
WHEREAS,\quad Peer-reviewed research establishes that survey items framed as evaluations of teaching effectiveness, course effectiveness, or instructor competence are susceptible to bias linked to the instructor's gender, race, and accent, and that such items do not reliably measure teaching effectiveness; and

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
WHEREAS,\quad Research establishes that open-ended comments in student evaluations show the strongest evidence of equity bias, including disproportionate commentary on women's appearance and personality, abusive and threatening language directed at women and academics from marginalized groups, and volume that makes institutional screening infeasible; and

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
WHEREAS,\quad The most useful feedback students can provide---what is working, what is not, what they wish were different---is most valuable while the course is still in progress, when the instructor can still act on it; such feedback is developmental, not evaluative, and belongs in a formative process distinct from the summative personnel file; and

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
WHEREAS,\quad The Ad Hoc Committee's report, ``The Student Perceptions of Learning Experience: Rationale and Broad Principles of Design,'' proposes a Student Perceptions of Learning Experience centered on experiential reports of class climate---asking students to report on their own experience in the classroom rather than to render evaluative judgments about teaching effectiveness---together with companion documents on scoring, reporting, and implementation, and a formative learning feedback process offered through the Center for Teaching, Learning and Technology; therefore, be it

\vspace{0.75em}
\noindent
RESOLVED:\quad That AS-759-13 is superseded by this resolution; and be it further

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
RESOLVED:\quad That the Academic Senate approve the Ad Hoc Committee's report ``The Student Perceptions of Learning Experience: Rationale and Broad Principles of Design'' and its companion documents on scoring and reporting, evaluator guidance, and implementation; and be it further

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
RESOLVED:\quad That the instrument currently known as the Student Evaluation of Instruction and Student Evaluation of Faculty be renamed the \textbf{Student Perceptions of Learning Experience}; and be it further

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
RESOLVED:\quad That the Student Perceptions of Learning Experience assess the following six aspects of class climate: Regard for Students, No Differential Enforcement of Expectations, and Access to Instructor and Instructor Resources (Interpersonal); Perceived Course Coherence (Structural); and Participatory Climate and Welcoming Learning Environment (Environmental); and be it further

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
RESOLVED:\quad That open-ended questions be discontinued from the summative component of the Student Perceptions of Learning Experience, and that unstructured student feedback be provided through the formative component, which is shared only with the instructor and is not included in the personnel file; and be it further

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
RESOLVED:\quad That the Center for Teaching, Learning and Technology develop a voluntary formative learning feedback process---distinct from the Student Perceptions of Learning Experience---designed to give instructors actionable information about how students are experiencing the learning environment while the course is still in progress; that this process be organized around research-based principles of learning; that it include open-ended questions as part of the formative feedback; that results be shared only with the instructor and not included in the personnel file, not reported to department chairs or deans, and not used for retention, tenure, promotion, or any other employment decision; and that no instructor be required to use it and no administrator may mandate its use; and be it further

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
RESOLVED:\quad That the University provide the Center for Teaching, Learning and Technology with sufficient resources---including staffing, technology infrastructure, and faculty development support---to develop, maintain, and offer the formative learning feedback process for the benefit of helping instructors improve the learning environment in their courses; and be it further

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
RESOLVED:\quad That colleges, departments, and/or programs may include additional questions in the Student Perceptions of Learning Experience, based on their respective faculty-based governance procedures, provided that such questions follow the guidance established in the evaluator's manual---specifically, the experiential-reporting principle and bias-minimizing design criteria; and be it further

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
RESOLVED:\quad That faculty members may include student evaluation questions for their own classes, and that all student responses (numeric and/or written) to faculty-generated questions may be excluded from inclusion in the faculty member's personnel action file (PAF) at the discretion of the faculty member; and be it further

\vspace{0.5em}
\noindent
RESOLVED:\quad That the Academic Senate designate the Instruction and Faculty Affairs Committees as the appropriate committees for making potential revisions to the Student Perceptions of Learning Experience in the future, and that these revisions are subject to approval by the Academic Senate.

\end{linenumbers}

\vspace{2em}
\noindent
\begin{tabular}{@{}ll}
Proposed by: & Ad Hoc Committee on Student Perception\\
             & of Teaching Effectiveness\\[0.5em]
Date:        & \textit{[date of first reading]}\\
\end{tabular}
```
